pr.title: More easy to implement functions in ftSk_SpecialHi.c
pr.author: Gelatart

summary: Implemented several previously stubbed Sheik Up-B functions in ftSk_SpecialHi.c, mostly by incorporating or adapting matching decomp.me scratches. The PR replaced placeholder markers with collision, animation, and motion-state transition logic for parts of SpecialHi/SpecialAirHi, including ground/air collision checks, landing/ground-to-air-fall transitions, animation end handling, and a ground collision fallback into a travel/transition state.

key_files.role: Implemented multiple previously stubbed Sheik Up-B helper/callback functions.

what_changed: Added a local static prototype for ft_8008A2BC(Fighter_GObj* gobj), used by the newly implemented ftSk_SpecialHi_Anim callback when the animation has no frames remaining.

Implemented ftSk_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Coll. It gets the Fighter, derives a signed direction from fp->facing_dir compared to ftSk_Init_804D9660, calls ft_CheckGroundAndLedge, transitions via ftSk_SpecialHi_80113390 on ground/ledge success, otherwise returns early if ftCliffCommon_80081298 succeeds.

Implemented ftSk_SpecialHi_8011374C, annotated as likely AS_SheikUpBGroundtoAirFall. It calls ftCommon_8007D60C, constructs motion flags from Ft_MF_* constants, changes to motion state 0x167 using fp->cur_anim_frame and zero animation parameters, then sets fp->x2223_b4 and fp->x221E_b0 true.

Implemented ftSk_SpecialHi_801137C8, annotated as AS_SheikUpBLand. It calls ftCommon_8007D7FC, builds the same grouped motion flag combination, changes to motion state 0x164 at the current animation frame, and sets fp->x221E_b0 true.
Implemented ftSk_SpecialHi_Anim. It uses FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 and calls ft_8008A2BC when ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining returns 0.
Implemented ftSk_SpecialHi_Coll. It calls ft_800827A0 and invokes ftSk_SpecialHi_80113E40 if the ground/collision helper returns 0.

Implemented ftSk_SpecialHi_80113E40. It calls ftCommon_8007D60C, changes motion state to 0x168 with flags 0x0C4C508A and Sheik constants ftSk_Init_804D9664/ftSk_Init_804D9660, then sets fp->accessory4_cb to fn_80113038.
smart_moves: The author explicitly credited decomp.me scratches for several functions, making provenance and matching confidence easier to audit later.

Motion flags in ftSk_SpecialHi_8011374C and ftSk_SpecialHi_801137C8 were expressed with named Ft_MF_* constants and comments showing the grouped hex values, preserving both readability and a direct comparison path to the raw expected bitmask.

The implementation reused existing fighter transition helpers such as ftCommon_8007D60C, ftCommon_8007D7FC, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, ft_CheckGroundAndLedge, and ftCliffCommon_80081298 rather than open-coding behavior.
FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 was used in ftSk_SpecialHi_Anim, indicating attention to stack layout/matching needs even for a very small animation callback.
The author kept uncertainty visible in comments, such as the tentative AS_SheikUpBGroundtoAirFall label and the note that one scratch had struct-definition warnings.
decomp_lessons: For fighter action-state functions, matching often depends on preserving exact callback signatures and using the project’s typed Fighter_GObj/HSD_GObj casts consistently at call sites.

When replacing placeholder decomp markers, keep decomp.me scratch URLs in nearby comments if they materially informed the implementation; this helps later reviewers or cleanup passes verify the source of a match.
Raw motion-state IDs can be made more reviewable by pairing them with known action-state comments such as AS_SheikUpBLand or AS_SheikUpBTravelAir, even when the code still uses numeric IDs.
Bitmask-heavy Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls are easier to maintain when expressed as named Ft_MF_* flags, especially when comments preserve the expected aggregate hex values.
Small callbacks may still need stack-padding macros like FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 to match; do not assume trivial animation-end handlers are automatically layout-insensitive.
Scratch code with struct warnings can still be useful, but the warning should be recorded so future struct/header cleanup can revisit whether the match relied on incomplete type information.
naming_conventions: Sheik-specific functions in this file use the ftSk_SpecialHi_* prefix, with action-state callback suffixes such as _Anim, _Phys, and _Coll.
Unidentified or address-based helper functions retain address-style names such as ftSk_SpecialHi_8011374C and ftSk_SpecialHi_80113E40.
Known action-state meanings were left as comments rather than fully renaming functions, for example AS_SheikUpBGroundtoAirFall, AS_SheikUpBLand, AS_SheikUpBTravelGround, and AS_SheikUpBTravelAir.
Fighter motion flag constants use the Ft_MF_* naming style, including Ft_MF_SkipModelPartVis, Ft_MF_SkipItemVis, Ft_MF_KeepGfx, Ft_MF_SkipHit, and Ft_MF_UpdateCmd.
Fighter bitfields are accessed using named generated-style fields such as fp->x2223_b4 and fp->x221E_b0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used decomp.me scratch-derived implementations for ftSk_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Coll, ftSk_SpecialHi_8011374C, ftSk_SpecialHi_801137C8, ftSk_SpecialHi_Anim, ftSk_SpecialHi_Coll, and ftSk_SpecialHi_80113E40.
Derived facing direction as an s32 with explicit -1/1 branches based on fp->facing_dir < ftSk_Init_804D9660 before calling ft_CheckGroundAndLedge.
Built the same motion flag value in two stages with named constants and comments for the intermediate groups 0xC4C and 0x508E, rather than using one opaque literal in the 0x164/0x167 transitions.
Used a literal combined flag value 0x0C4C508AU in ftSk_SpecialHi_80113E40 where that appears to be the matching form for the motion state 0x168 transition.
Inserted FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 in ftSk_SpecialHi_Anim to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Preserved current animation frame via fp->cur_anim_frame in multiple Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls.

review_feedback.text: The context reports one issue comment but provides no comment excerpt, so no specific reviewer feedback can be extracted.
No review comments were present in the provided PR context.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching ftSk_SpecialHi_80113838 and ftSk_SpecialHi_80113A30, which remained placeholder stubs in this diff excerpt.
Search for cleanup PRs that replace numeric Sheik motion state IDs 0x164, 0x167, and 0x168 with named action-state constants.
Investigate whether the struct/header warnings mentioned for the ftSk_SpecialHi_801137C8 scratch led to later Fighter struct or bitfield refinements.
Compare other fighter SpecialHi implementations for the same Ft_MF_* flag grouping pattern and FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 usage in animation callbacks.
